Muskmelon: A juicy shield against heat and dehydration
Regular consumption of muskmelon can not only keep you hydrated but also boosts immunity, and is good for skin health
Muskmelon is a refreshing summer fruit loved for its sweet taste, high water content and impressive nutritional value. Widely consumed during summer months, it helps keep the body cool and hydrated while offering a range of health benefits.
Nature’s nutrient-rich bounty
Rich in vitamins A and C, muskmelon supports healthy skin, boosts immunity and improves vision. It is also a good source of dietary fibre, which aids digestion and prevents constipation. The fruit contains antioxidants that help fight free radicals in the body, reducing the risk of chronic diseases. Its high water content — over 90 per cent — makes it excellent for maintaining hydration, especially in hot climates. Additionally, muskmelon is low in calories, making it a great choice for those looking to manage weight.
A study published in the ‘Journal of Agricultural and Food Chemistry’ found that muskmelon contains high levels of vitamin C, beta-carotene and polyphenolic compounds. These bioactive compounds show strong antioxidant activity, which helps protect the body from oxidative stress and supports skin-cell repair and overall skin health. Its high vitamin C content helps in collagen production, which keeps the skin firm, youthful and wrinkle-free. Regular consumption of muskmelon can therefore help reduce signs of ageing such as fine lines and dullness. It also contains vitamin A, which supports skin repair and helps maintain a smooth and even texture.
Enjoy it as snack or smoothie
It can be enjoyed in various ways. It is commonly eaten fresh as a snack, added to fruit salads, or blended into juices and smoothies. The seeds of muskmelon are also edible and are often dried and consumed for their nutritional benefits, including healthy fats and proteins. Making it a part of your daily diet, especially during summer, can promote overall health and well-being.
Best time to consume
The best time to eat muskmelon is during the morning or early afternoon. Eating it on an empty stomach can help with better digestion and nutrient absorption. Since it is light and hydrating, it works well as a breakfast fruit or a mid-morning snack. However, it is generally advised not to consume muskmelon late at night, as its high-water content may cause discomfort or frequent urination, potentially disturbing sleep.
